name: phylib

metadata.name: phylib
metadata.summary: Ephys data analysis for thousands of channels

documentation:
# phylib
[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/travis/cortex-lab/phylib.svg)](https://travis-ci.org/cortex-lab/phylib)
[![codecov.io](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/cortex-lab/phylib.svg)](http://codecov.io/github/cortex-lab/phylib?branch=master)

Electrophysiological data analysis library used by [phy](https://github.com/kwikteam/phy/), a spike sorting visualization software, and [ibllib](https://github.com/int-brain-lab/ibllib/).

functions.name: phylib.io.model._compute_pcs
documentation:
Compute the PCs of an array x, where each row is an observation.
x can be a 2D or 3D array. In the latter case, the PCs are computed
and concatenated iteratively along the last axis.

functions.name: phylib.io.model._project_pcs
documentation:
Project data points onto principal components.
Arguments:

  * pcs: the PCs as returned by `compute_pcs`.

methods.name: phylib.io.model.TemplateModel.get_amplitudes_true
documentation:
Convert spike amplitude values to input amplitudes units
via scaling by unwhitened template waveform.
:param sample2unit float: factor to convert the raw data to a physical unit (defaults 1.)
:returns: spike_amplitudes_volts: np.array [nspikes] spike amplitudes in raw data units
:returns: templates_volts: np.array[ntemplates, nsamples, nchannels]: templates
in raw data units
:returns: template_amps_volts: np.array[ntemplates]: average templates amplitudes
 in raw data units
To scale the template for template matching,
raw_data_volts = templates_volts * spike_amplitudes_volts / template_amps_volts

class_variables.name: phylib.io.model.TemplateModel.n_closest_channels
documentation:
Fraction of the peak amplitude required by the closest channels to be kept as best
channels.

Object holding all data of a KiloSort/phy dataset.

Constructor
-----------

dir_path : str or Path
    Path to the dataset directory
dat_path : str, Path, or list
    Path to the raw data files.
dtype : NumPy dtype
    Data type of the raw data file
offset : int
    Header offset of the binary file
n_channels_dat : int
    Number of channels in the dat file
sample_rate : float
    Sampling rate of the data file.

functions.name: phylib.io.datasets.download_file
documentation:
Download a binary file from an URL.

The checksum will be downloaded from `URL + .md5`. If this download
succeeds, the file's MD5 will be compared to the expected checksum.

Parameters
----------

url : str
    The file's URL.
output_path : str
    The path where the file is to be saved.

Write the merged spike clusters, and register self.cluster_offsets.

We load all cluster metadata from TSV files, renumber the clusters,

methods.name: phylib.io.merge.Merger.write_misc
documentation:
Write misc merged data.

Similar templates: we make a block diagonal matrix from the n_templates * n_templates
matrices, assuming no similarity between templates from different probes.

Whitening matrix: same thing, except that the matrices are n_channels * n_channels.

methods.name: phylib.io.merge.Merger.merge
documentation: Merge the probes data and return a TemplateModel instance of the merged data.

Merge spike-sorted data from different probes and output datasets to disk.

Constructor
-----------

subdirs : list
    List of paths to the probe directories.
out_dir : str or Path
    Output directory for the merged spike-sorted data.
probe_info : list
    For each probe, a dictionary with the following fields:
        * `label`: a string with the probe label (defaults to the probe folder name)
        * any other field, such as `model`, `serial_number`, etc.

    All fields will be saved in `probes.description.tsv`.
Main Merger class

modules.name: phylib.io.array
documentation: Utility functions for NumPy arrays.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: phylib.io.array._clip
documentation: Utility functions

functions.name: phylib.io.array._range_from_slice
documentation: Convert a slice to an array of integers.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._unique
documentation:
Faster version of np.unique().

This version is restricted to 1D arrays of non-negative integers.

It is only faster if len(x) >> len(unique(x)).

functions.name: phylib.io.array._normalize
documentation: Normalize an array into [0, 1].

functions.name: phylib.io.array._index_of
documentation:
Replace scalars in an array by their indices in a lookup table.

Implicitely assume that:

* All elements of arr and lookup are non-negative integers.
* All elements or arr belong to lookup.

This is not checked for performance reasons.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._pad
documentation:
Pad an array with zeros along the first axis.

Parameters
----------

n : int
    Size of the returned array in the first axis.
dir : str
    Direction of the padding. Must be one 'left' or 'right'.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._get_padded
documentation:
Return `data[start:end]` filling in with zeros outside array bounds

Assumes that either `start<0` or `end>len(data)` but not both.

functions.name: phylib.io.array.get_closest_clusters
documentation:
Return a list of pairs `(cluster, similarity)` sorted by decreasing
similarity to a given cluster.

functions.name: phylib.io.array.read_array
documentation: Read a .npy array.
I/O functions

functions.name: phylib.io.array.write_array
documentation: Write an array to a .npy file.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._excerpt_step
documentation:
Compute the step of an excerpt set as a function of the number
of excerpts or their sizes.
Chunking functions

functions.name: phylib.io.array.chunk_bounds
documentation:
Return chunk bounds.

Chunks have the form:

    [ overlap/2 | chunk_size-overlap | overlap/2 ]
    s_start   keep_start           keep_end     s_end

Except for the first and last chunks which do not have a left/right
overlap.

This generator yields (s_start, s_end, keep_start, keep_end).

functions.name: phylib.io.array.excerpts
documentation: Yield (start, end) where start is included and end is excluded.

functions.name: phylib.io.array.data_chunk
documentation: Get a data chunk.

functions.name: phylib.io.array.get_excerpts
documentation: Return excerpts of a data array.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._spikes_in_clusters
documentation: Return the ids of all spikes belonging to the specified clusters.
Spike clusters utility functions

functions.name: phylib.io.array._spikes_per_cluster
documentation: Return a dictionary {cluster: list_of_spikes}.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._flatten_per_cluster
documentation: Convert a dictionary {cluster: spikes} to a spikes array.

functions.name: phylib.io.array.grouped_mean
documentation:
Compute the mean of a spike-dependent quantity for every cluster.

The two arguments should be 1D array with `n_spikes` elements.

The output is a 1D array with `n_clusters` elements. The clusters are
sorted in increasing order.

functions.name: phylib.io.array._times_in_chunks
documentation: Return the indices of the times that belong to a list of kept chunks.
Spike selection

Select a given number of spikes per cluster among a subset of the chunks.

functions.name: phylib.io.traces._get_subitems

Given a list of part/chunk bounds and an item passed to __getitem__(), return
a list (part_or_chunk_idx, subitem) to be concatenated over the first axis.
subitem is either a slice or a list/array of indices.

https://github.com/cortex-lab/phylib/blob/master/phylib/io/array.py

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
flake8: noqa

modules.name: phylib.stats.ccg
documentation: Cross-correlograms.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: phylib.stats.ccg._increment
documentation:
Increment some indices in a 1D vector of non-negative integers.
Repeated indices are taken into account.
Cross-correlograms

functions.name: phylib.stats.ccg._symmetrize_correlograms
documentation: Return the symmetrized version of the CCG arrays.

functions.name: phylib.stats.ccg.firing_rate
documentation: Compute the average number of spikes per cluster per bin.

functions.name: phylib.stats.ccg.correlograms
documentation:
Compute all pairwise cross-correlograms among the clusters appearing
in `spike_clusters`.

Parameters
----------

spike_times : array-like
    Spike times in seconds.
spike_clusters : array-like
    Spike-cluster mapping.
cluster_ids : array-like
    The list of *all* unique clusters, in any order. That order will be used
    in the output array.
bin_size : float
    Size of the bin, in seconds.
window_size : float
    Size of the window, in seconds.
sample_rate : float
    Sampling rate.
symmetrize : boolean (True)
    Whether the output matrix should be symmetrized or not.

Returns
-------

correlograms : array
    A `(n_clusters, n_clusters, winsize_samples)` array with all pairwise CCGs.

* Probe specified: one can change the current channel_group.

modules.name: phylib.utils.event
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.set_silent
documentation: Set whether to silence the events.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.reset
documentation: Remove all registered callbacks.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter._get_on_name
documentation: Return `eventname` when the function name is `on_<eventname>()`.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.silent
documentation:
Prevent all callbacks to be called if events are raised
in the context manager.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.connect
documentation:
Register a callback function to a given event.

To register a callback function to the `spam` event, where `obj` is
an instance of a class deriving from `EventEmitter`:

```python
@obj.connect(sender=sender)
def on_spam(sender, arg1, arg2):
    pass
```

This is called when `obj.emit('spam', sender, arg1, arg2)` is called.

Several callback functions can be registered for a given event.

The registration order is conserved and may matter in applications.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.unconnect
documentation: Unconnect specified callback functions or senders.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.EventEmitter.emit
documentation:
Call all callback functions registered with an event.

Any positional and keyword arguments can be passed here, and they will
be forwarded to the callback functions.

Return the list of callback return results.
Event system

classes.name: phylib.utils.event.PartialFormatter

documentation: Format a field.

Progress reporter

classes.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.set_progress_message
documentation:
Set a progress message.

The string needs to contain `{progress}`.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.set_complete_message
documentation: Set a complete message.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.increment
documentation:
Equivalent to `self.value += 1`.

Custom keywoard arguments can also be passed to be processed in the
progress message format string.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.reset
documentation: Reset the value to 0 and the value max to a given value.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.value
documentation: Current value (integer).

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.value_max
documentation: Maximum value (integer).

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.is_complete
documentation: Return whether the task has completed.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.set_complete
documentation: Set the task as complete.

methods.name: phylib.utils.event.ProgressReporter.progress
documentation: Return the current progress as a float value in `[0, 1]`.

A class that reports progress done.

Example
-------

```python
pr = ProgressReporter()
pr.set_progress_message("Progress: {progress}%...")
pr.set_complete_message("Completed!")
pr.value_max = 10

for i in range(10):
    pr.value += 1  # or pr.increment()
```

You can also add custom keyword arguments in `pr.increment()`: these
will be replaced in the message string.

Emits
-----

* `progress(value, value_max)`
* `complete()`
